title: Unit 6 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Adjectives, Colors & Numbers

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 6: Adjectives, Colors and Numbers. In English... adjectives are static. A 'good' boy... a 'good' girl. The word never changes. But in Greek... adjectives are chameleons. They must match their nouns perfectly in Gender... Number... and Case. This is called 'Agreement'. I'm William... and here is Athina. Athina... how does this agreement sound in a real sentence?
To achieve agreement... the adjective often rhymes with the article and the noun! Listen to the
It's a perfect rhyme. But what happens when we move these into the Object case... the Accusative we learned in Unit 5?
They hunt in packs! Just like masculine nouns drop their
when they are the object... the masculine adjective must do the same.
So they always stay in sync. Now... let's apply this to the world of colors. Colors are just adjectives! Athina... give us the primary colors.
the red apple... using the neuter ending to match the apple.
But wait... there’s a group of 'Rebel Colors' in our lesson. They refuse to change their shape!
Yes! Colors borrowed from other languages like
ο μπλε ουρανός... η μπλε θάλασσα... το μπλε αυτοκίνητο.
If you're feeling lazy... just describe everything as blue! Finally... let’s talk numbers. From one to ten. Athina... take us through them.
Ένας... Δύο... Τρία... Τέσσερα... Πέντε... Έξι... Εφτά... Οχτώ... Εννέα... Δέκα.
Wait... I recognize that first one.
It’s exactly the same as the indefinite articles we learned in Unit 2! It’s 'One man'... 'One woman'... 'One child'.
The number ONE is a chameleon too. It must match the gender of what you are counting. And a little secret: the numbers Three and Four...
also have different forms for masculine and feminine... but for now... just learn the basic counting forms.

Focus on 1 to 10 for now. You have now completed the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 6. You can now describe the world in color... count your change... and ensure your sentences are in perfect agreement. Review the colors and numbers vocabulary and the mastery checklist. Excellent work... we’ll see you in Unit 7: Asking Questions.
Συγχαρητήρια! Τα λέμε στο επόμενο μάθημα.
